records.ety: [L. appetentia, fr. appetere to strive after, long for. See Appetite.]

defs: Fixed and strong desire; esp. natural desire; a craving; an eager appetite.
quotes: They had a strong appetency for reading.

defs: Specifically: An instinctive inclination or propensity in animals to perform certain actions, as in the young to suck, in aquatic fowls to enter into water and to swim; the tendency of an organized body to seek what satisfies the wants of its organism.
quotes: These lacteals have mouths, and by animal selection or appetency the absorb such part of the fluid as is agreeable to their palate.

defs: Natural tendency; affinity; attraction; -- used of inanimate objects.
